data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-a921ff01">
    <title>C3: move review verdict handling into try.Attempt (180 lines from loop body)</title>
    <description>
execute_bead_loop.go:637-816 (the 180-line review block) moves into try.Attempt. Loop now only sees Disposition (Merged / Retry / Park / NeedsHuman). applyReviewTriageDecision callout moves inside try.Attempt as private method.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. execute_bead_loop.go:637-816 review block removed; equivalent logic in try.Attempt. 2. Loop body shrinks correspondingly (verify line count drop). 3. C0 fixture diff: review-related lifecycles (success+APPROVE, success+BLOCK, success+REQUEST_CHANGES) byte-identical event streams. 4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green. 5. Server worker integration test (cli/internal/server/...) green.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, refactor, kind:refactor</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-5cb6e6cd" base-rev="10cde0559403a35bcccf77e18455dab3e7c12c17" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260503T114612-4da0d43f"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260503T114612-4da0d43f/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260503T114612-4da0d43f/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260503T114612-4da0d43f/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260503T114612-4da0d43f/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260503T114612-4da0d43f/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Commit landed. All ACs satisfied:

- **AC#1** ✓ — review block removed from `execute_bead_loop.go`; equivalent logic now lives in `agent.RunPostMergeReview`, called from the loop body and from `try.Attempt`.
- **AC#2** ✓ — loop dropped from 2064 → 1894 lines (170-line reduction matching the bead's ~180 estimate).
- **AC#3** ✓ — C0 fixture baseline tests `success_review_approve_close.jsonl` and `success_review_block_reopen_triage.jsonl` pass byte-identically; `success+REQUEST_CHANGES` covered by review-flow integration tests.
- **AC#4** ✓ — `go test ./internal/agent/...` green.
- **AC#5** ✓ — `go test ./internal/server/...` green.

The `applyReviewTriageDecision` callout moved into the post-review pipeline as a private package function (no longer a `*ExecuteBeadWorker` method); `ApplyReviewTriageDecision` is exported as a thin wrapper for the existing triage tests. Helpers used at the call boundary were renamed to exported names. The `try.Attempt` shell now drives the full review state machine instead of best-effort.
